metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for prax branch

bypass.reason: Test functions follow pytest naming conventions (test_*). Docstrings on test functions are non-standard in this codebase — only new tests added in DPLAN-0125 Track G have them. Pre-existing 36 functions comply with project test style.
Test files live in tests/ by convention, not in the 3-layer apps/ structure. This is a false positive for the test directory.
Bootstrap/lifecycle logging - print() is used before the logging system is initialized and during shutdown. Logger cannot use itself for initialization messages (circular dependency).
This IS the display handler layer. unified_stream.py is the designated terminal output formatter for the monitoring system. console.print() is its core responsibility, not a violation.
Display handler for file change reporting. reporter.py formats and renders file change events to terminal using Rich markup. console.print() is its designated purpose.

Prax logging infrastructure — this file IS part of the logging system. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency). stdlib logging.getLogger() is the correct pattern for logging infrastructure internals.
Prax logging infrastructure — directly imported by logger.py (import chain). Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax logging infrastructure — directly imported by logger.py (import chain). Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax logging infrastructure — part of the terminal output subsystem within handlers/logging/. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py line 69. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — imported transitively by logger.py via discovery/filtering.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py line 75. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py line 64. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — imported by logging/lifecycle.py which is in logger.py's dependency graph. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py line 68. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — imported by discovery/watcher.py which is in logger.py's dependency graph. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency). Logs route correctly via stdlib logger with prax naming.
Prax import chain — transitively imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — imported via logging/lifecycle.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax import chain — imported via discovery/watcher.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Architectural — config/load.py provides shared constants (ECOSYSTEM_ROOT, paths) used by all prax handlers. It is shared infrastructure, not an encapsulation violation.
Architectural — imports ignore patterns config. config/ is shared infrastructure within prax.
Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure) and trigger.modules.core (optional cross-branch integration with graceful fallback).
Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure), trigger (optional integration), and uses module_name parameter for explicit caller attribution in monitoring context.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for explicit branch detection context. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() is not appropriate for a detection utility that receives context from callers.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for explicit module tracking. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() would defeat the purpose of a tracker that receives module names from external sources.
Architectural — integrates with watcher/monitor.py for file watching coordination. Both are part of the monitoring subsystem.
Architectural — optional trigger integration for file system events. Import has graceful fallback if trigger is unavailable.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for display attribution. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() would resolve to the display thread, not the originating module.
Architectural — imports PRAX_ROOT, ECOSYSTEM_ROOT from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports PRAX_ROOT from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports _find_repo_root from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — logging infrastructure imports config.load for path resolution. Part of prax logging internals.

Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure) and uses module_name parameter for explicit direct-logging binding. Auto-detection is the default (get_direct_logger()), but the internal class accepts explicit names for infrastructure callers.
Architectural — imports PRAX_JSON_DIR from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports discovery/scanner for module discovery during logging lifecycle management. Uses module_name parameter for explicit lifecycle control of named modules.

Architectural — logging infrastructure imports config.load for path configuration. Uses module_name parameter for explicit logger setup per-module. Auto-detection is not appropriate for a setup function that configures loggers for specified modules.
Architectural — module_name parameter in get_module_logs_dir() receives explicit module names for log path resolution. This is the config API — callers pass module names, it resolves paths.
Architectural — imports _find_repo_root from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports PRAX_JSON_DIR from config.load (shared infrastructure). Uses module_name parameter for explicit filter context from callers.

json_handler.py IS the JSON template management system for prax. load_template() and json_templates/ are its core functionality, not a violation of the template-free standard. This file provides the template infrastructure that other files consume.

Monitor is a 3-thread architecture (display, file watcher, log watcher) that is tightly coupled by design. The threads share state (queues, positions dict, stop events) making splitting impractical without introducing complex inter-module synchronization. Direct JSON read of registry at line 344 is a one-time startup operation for branch discovery — routing through json_handler would add unnecessary dependency for a simple registry read.
False positive — print(ok) is inside a subprocess script string passed to python -c, not a bare print() call in this module's execution context.
logger.py IS the prax logger module — it cannot import itself. 'Prax logger import not found' is expected.
agent_status.py manages agent status tracking independently of the prax logging pipeline. Uses get_direct_logger() for its own logging needs.
False positive — stdlib imports (lines 49-55) are correctly before aipass imports (lines 58+). Checker misidentifies docstring content as import lines.
False positive — multiline function signatures cause the docstring checker to miss existing docstrings.
False positive — property getter/setter and watchdog callback methods with multiline signatures.
False positive — placeholder class methods inside except block detected as missing docstrings.
False positive — multiline function signature causes docstring checker to miss existing docstring.
False positive — multiline function signature causes docstring checker to miss existing docstring.
False positive — __all__ is a Python convention (PEP 8), not a user-defined constant requiring UPPER_CASE.
False positive — __all__ is a Python convention (PEP 8), not a user-defined constant requiring UPPER_CASE.
False positive — _monitoring_active is a mutable threading flag (threading.Event), not a constant.
Redundant prefix in filename — monitoring_filters.py in monitoring/ dir. Renaming to filters.py would break existing imports across multiple files. Deferred to avoid churn.
False positive — start_monitoring, stop_monitoring, MonitoringEvent are re-exported imports at module level, not constants. They are function/class references imported for external consumers.
False positive — _trigger_available is a mutable flag set conditionally in try/except, not a constant.

False positive — logger_key, target, sys_limits, backupCount, encoding, local_limits, key are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants. backupCount follows logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler parameter naming.
False positive — module_name and counter are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants.

Architectural — _original_getLogger and _original_basicConfig store references to stdlib functions (camelCase follows stdlib naming). enhanced_getLogger wraps logging.getLogger (matching stdlib convention).
False positive — _terminal_output_enabled and _terminal_module_available are mutable flags set conditionally, not constants.
on_created() is a watchdog.FileSystemEventHandler callback — called by watchdog.Observer at runtime, not by name in branch code.
on_created(), on_modified(), on_deleted(), on_moved() are watchdog.FileSystemEventHandler callbacks — called by watchdog.Observer at runtime via method dispatch.
on_modified() is a watchdog.FileSystemEventHandler callback — called by watchdog.Observer at runtime when log files change.
on_created(), on_modified(), on_deleted(), on_moved() are watchdog.FileSystemEventHandler callbacks — called by watchdog.Observer at runtime via method dispatch.
format() is a logging.Formatter.format() override — called by the Python logging framework when formatting log records, not by name in branch code.
run_monitoring_loop() is the handler entry point for continuous monitoring — designed to be called by the logger module or daemon processes as a long-running loop.

initialize_logging_system() and shutdown_logging_system() are public API lifecycle functions exported in __all__ — designed to be called by other branches and entry points to start/stop the logging system.
False positive — 'changed' is a local variable inside _apply_structural_updates(), not a module-level constant.

Intentional — status is a leaf command where bare invocation shows system status (the primary user intent), not module introspection. Introspection is available via print_introspection() and __main__.
Template files managed by spawn — prax cannot add/remove template files. Template structure is defined in spawn/templates/builder/ and applied at branch creation time.

queue.Empty is expected control flow from PriorityQueue.get(timeout=N), not an error. Logging it produces 144,000 noise entries per 4hrs of monitoring (10 polls/sec). The empty state IS the normal idle state.
Test files live in tests/ by convention, not in the 3-layer apps/ structure. This is a false positive for the test directory.
Test file imports logging handlers directly for testing purposes. Line 174 imports from aipass.prax.apps.handlers.logging.introspection to test that module's public API.
Test file helper function fake_sleep() is a test fixture helper. Test fixture docstrings are optional in this codebase per pytest conventions.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, error_handling, log_visibility, handlers, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass

architectural_notes.prax_import_chain: logger.py imports from: logging/setup.py, logging/introspection.py, logging/override.py, logging/direct.py, discovery/watcher.py, registry/load.py, config/load.py, json/json_handler.py. These files (and their transitive imports) cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger without circular dependency.
architectural_notes.config_as_shared_infra: config/load.py provides shared constants (ECOSYSTEM_ROOT, PRAX_ROOT, path helpers) used by nearly all prax handlers. It is intentionally shared infrastructure, not an encapsulation boundary.
architectural_notes.trigger_integration: Several handlers optionally import trigger.modules.core for event firing. All have graceful ImportError fallbacks. This is cross-branch integration, not an architectural violation.
